comprehension_gate.instruction: MANDATORY FIRST ACTION: Before any branch creation, file edits, or commands, read this entire prompt and all required attachments in full. Then present a structured comprehension summary to the Lead Architect covering: (a) corrective objective in your own words (b) work items with 1 sentence each (c) files to READ and files to WRITE (d) exact structural defects being corrected (e) ORACLE strategy for this DOCS-ONLY corrective milestone (f) risks or ambiguities (g) EXACT deliverable structure, recited VERBATIM from this prompt: - branch to create: feature/p5-task7-ea1-audit-correction from main at 488b198 - files to WRITE: docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md and docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md only - docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md must retain exactly these 6 top-level sections in this order: 1. ## Coverage Map 2. ## Stale Test Inventory 3. ## Assertion Quality Findings 4. ## Boundary Violations 5. ## Prioritized Gap Report 6. ## Pre-existing Skip Analysis - sections 1-4 must each contain the exact plain subsection heading: ### policy_agent - the two misfiled findings must be reclassified out of ## Boundary Violations: * Exact escalation floor boundary (0.50) untested * RateLimiter sliding window expiry untested - if no true boundary-correctness violations remain after re-review, the boundary subsection must say exactly: No material boundary violations identified for policy_agent. - append Entry 40 for this correction milestone Then STOP and WAIT for explicit Lead Architect approval. Do NOT create the branch, write files, or run commands until the Lead Architect confirms that your recitation is correct.

milestone.title: Task 7 / EA-1 Correction: Policy Agent Audit Structural and Taxonomy Fix
milestone.model_rationale: Constraint-heavy DOCS-ONLY correction against a merged artifact with strict structural requirements and non-dev ORACLE checks.
milestone.predecessor: Main HEAD 488b198 contains Entry 39 and the merged EA-1 artifact, but does NOT include EA-2. This correction milestone reserves Entry 40 on the correction branch.
milestone.objective: Correct the merged EA-1 policy_agent audit artifact so it complies with the original EA-1 prompt contract and passes the independent spot-check gate. This is not a new full-scope audit and not a code-change milestone. You must repair structural non-compliance in docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md, re-evaluate policy_agent boundary correctness against the actual contract, reclassify the two misfiled value-boundary findings into the coverage taxonomy, normalize Entry 39 metadata, and append a new correction record as Entry 40. No production files or test files may be changed.

correction_basis.item: Original EA-1 prompt required the exact plain subsection heading ### policy_agent under sections 1-4; the merged artifact does not contain those headings.
The current policy_agent content under ## Boundary Violations is not boundary-correctness analysis. It contains value-boundary coverage gaps, which are misclassified under the Task 7 contract.
Independent oversight confirmed the two misclassified findings are factually real but belong in coverage analysis, not in boundary-correctness findings.
Entry 39 metadata on main is not fully contract-compliant: title differs from the original prompt contract, Type is missing, and Disposition is recorded as PASS rather than COMPLETE.

milestone.scope:
Re-read these policy_agent production files: - services/policy_agent/src/adjudicator.py - services/policy_agent/src/boot.py - services/policy_agent/src/car.py - services/policy_agent/src/config_loader.py - services/policy_agent/src/constants.py - services/policy_agent/src/entrypoint.py - services/policy_agent/src/gpu_inference.py - services/policy_agent/src/ipc.py - services/policy_agent/src/jwt_minter.py - services/policy_agent/src/rule_engine.py
Re-read these policy_agent test files: - services/policy_agent/tests/test_adjudicator.py - services/policy_agent/tests/test_boot.py - services/policy_agent/tests/test_car.py - services/policy_agent/tests/test_config_loader.py - services/policy_agent/tests/test_entrypoint.py - services/policy_agent/tests/test_gpu_inference.py - services/policy_agent/tests/test_hybrid_adjudicator.py - services/policy_agent/tests/test_ipc.py - services/policy_agent/tests/test_jwt_minter.py - services/policy_agent/tests/test_rate_and_resource_rules.py - services/policy_agent/tests/test_rule_engine.py - services/policy_agent/tests/test_integration_car_pipeline.py
The purpose of the re-read is narrow: - verify policy_agent boundary correctness against the real contract - confirm the two misfiled findings remain valid as coverage findings - recalibrate any impacted coverage-level wording if the reclassification requires it - avoid reopening stale, assertion, or coverage findings that are not implicated by the correction

work_items.item: Re-read all 10 policy_agent production files and all 12 policy_agent test files before changing the artifact.
Restructure docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md so sections 1-4 each contain the exact plain heading ### policy_agent.
Demote the existing numbered EA-1 subheadings beneath ### policy_agent so the document preserves detail without violating the original section contract.
Reclassify the two misfiled Section 4 findings into the coverage taxonomy and update impacted module coverage wording or coverage levels if evidence requires it.
Rewrite the policy_agent boundary subsection so it contains only true boundary-correctness findings under the Task 7 contract.
Replace obsolete internal numbered cross-references in the EA-1 policy_agent content with descriptive text where needed.
Normalize Entry 39 metadata to the original EA-1 contract.
Append Entry 40 documenting this correction milestone.
Enforce strict DOC_ONLY hygiene: stage and commit only the two allowed docs files.
Use Tier 3 fail-safe if the correction cannot be completed without quality degradation.

deliverable.primary_artifact: Modify docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md in place on main-derived state. Do not introduce assistant_orchestrator or semantic_router content in this correction branch.
deliverable.exact_structure:
docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md must continue to contain exactly these 6 top-level sections in this exact order: 1. ## Coverage Map 2. ## Stale Test Inventory 3. ## Assertion Quality Findings 4. ## Boundary Violations 5. ## Prioritized Gap Report 6. ## Pre-existing Skip Analysis
Sections 1-4 must each contain the exact plain subsection heading: - ### policy_agent
Under each ### policy_agent subsection, you MAY retain supporting detail via lower-level headings such as #### Module Coverage Summary or #### Coverage Gaps by Module. Do not leave numbered level-3 headings directly under the top-level sections without ### policy_agent above them.
Sections 5 and 6 must remain exactly: - Deferred to EA-5 synthesis.
deliverable.coverage_contract: Under ## Coverage Map / ### policy_agent: - preserve the overall policy_agent coverage analysis - move the two misclassified findings out of ## Boundary Violations and into the appropriate coverage area - use these exact descriptive labels for verification stability: * #### Exact Escalation Floor Boundary (0.50) Untested * #### RateLimiter Sliding Window Expiry Untested - if the reclassification means `adjudicator.py` or `rule_engine.py` should no longer be described as COMPREHENSIVE, update the table and rationale accordingly - remove or update any coverage text that points readers to the old Section 4 placement
deliverable.stale_contract: Under ## Stale Test Inventory / ### policy_agent, preserve the accepted stale-test findings. This correction should alter structure only unless a descriptive cross-reference must be updated for internal consistency.
deliverable.assertion_contract: Under ## Assertion Quality Findings / ### policy_agent, preserve the accepted assertion-quality findings. Remove obsolete numbered cross-references if they no longer read correctly after the structural correction.
deliverable.boundary_contract:
Under ## Boundary Violations / ### policy_agent, evaluate only true boundary-correctness issues: - real socket connections - real filesystem writes beyond acceptable isolated scaffolding - dependence on live services, hardware, or manual setup - tests that should really be integration tests rather than service-level unit tests
The following do NOT belong in this section: - exact numeric threshold coverage gaps - untested value boundaries - missing edge-case assertions that are really coverage findings
If, after re-review of the in-scope files, no material policy_agent boundary violations exist, write exactly: No material boundary violations identified for policy_agent.
You may follow that exact sentence with a short evidence note, but do not reinsert the two misclassified value-boundary findings here.
deliverable.ledger_contract:
In docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md:
1. Normalize Entry 39 to the original EA-1 contract: - Title: Entry 39 — Task 7: Test Quality Audit / EA-1 Policy Agent Audit (COMPLETE) - Type: AUDIT / DOCS-ONLY - Disposition: COMPLETE
2. Append Entry 40 for this correction milestone.
Required Entry 40 content: - Title: Entry 40 - Task 7/EA-1 Correction: Policy Agent Audit Structural and Taxonomy Fix (COMPLETE) - Date: 2026-04-18 or actual execution date - Branch: feature/p5-task7-ea1-audit-correction - Predecessor: Entry 39 - Task 7: Test Quality Audit / EA-1 Policy Agent Audit - Type: AUDIT / DOCS-ONLY - Disposition: COMPLETE - Summary: corrected the merged EA-1 artifact to restore ### policy_agent subsection structure, reclassified value-boundary coverage findings out of ## Boundary Violations, normalized Entry 39 metadata, and preserved sections 5-6 as deferred to EA-5 - Files: docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md and docs/POST_OPERATIONAL_MATURATION_LEDGER.md only

negative_constraints.item: DO NOT modify any file under services/policy_agent/src/.
DO NOT modify any file under services/policy_agent/tests/.
DO NOT add assistant_orchestrator content to this correction branch.
DO NOT add semantic_router content to this correction branch.
DO NOT add top-level sections beyond the 6 required sections.
DO NOT rename any of the 6 required top-level sections.
DO NOT populate ## Prioritized Gap Report with findings. Leave it exactly: Deferred to EA-5 synthesis.
DO NOT populate ## Pre-existing Skip Analysis with findings. Leave it exactly: Deferred to EA-5 synthesis.
DO NOT leave the two misclassified value-boundary findings under ## Boundary Violations.
DO NOT leave sections 1-4 without the exact plain heading ### policy_agent.
DO NOT run coverage tooling or install dependencies.
DO NOT touch unrelated pre-existing untracked files in the worktree.

gate.criteria: No Python files may be created or modified in this milestone.

Do not run pytest as a standard gate for this slice. Use file inspection as the primary evidence source. If you run a targeted read-only command to confirm a reference or boundary claim, report it explicitly in your session close-out.

gate.command: Manual correction review against all 10 policy_agent production files and all 12 policy_agent test files in scope
gate.criteria: Boundary correctness is re-evaluated from source evidence, not assumed from the prior artifact. The two misclassified findings are confirmed as real coverage issues before reclassification.

The document contains the same 6 required top-level sections in the same order. Sections 1-4 each contain the exact plain heading ### policy_agent. Sections 5 and 6 remain exactly: Deferred to EA-5 synthesis.

gate.command: Review policy_agent coverage and boundary subsection content
gate.criteria: The exact labels #### Exact Escalation Floor Boundary (0.50) Untested and #### RateLimiter Sliding Window Expiry Untested appear under ## Coverage Map / ### policy_agent. Those findings do not remain under ## Boundary Violations. If no true boundary violations exist, the boundary subsection contains the exact sentence: No material boundary violations identified for policy_agent.
Entry 39 title, Type, and Disposition match the original EA-1 contract. Entry 40 exists and documents this correction milestone.

If the correction cannot be completed without degraded quality, STOP, leave a clearly labeled partial correction, record partial status in Entry 40, and recommend a re-scope instead of forcing a weak close-out.

verification.instruction: The Lead Architect will verify this corrective milestone using terminal commands only. Your final EA response must report results that these commands can confirm.

commands.expected: Single EA-1 correction commit on the feature branch
commands.command: Select-String -Path docs/TEST_AUDIT_FINDINGS.md -Pattern "^## ","^### policy_agent","^#### Exact Escalation Floor Boundary \\(0\\.50\\) Untested$","^#### RateLimiter Sliding Window Expiry Untested$","^No material boundary violations identified for policy_agent\\.$","Deferred to EA-5 synthesis"
commands.expected: Shows the 6 required top-level sections, the four required ### policy_agent subsections, the two reclassified coverage findings, the corrected boundary statement, and the unchanged deferred placeholders.
Entry 39 metadata is normalized and Entry 40 exists for the correction milestone.

commit_template.message:
Task 7 EA-1 correction: fix policy agent audit structure and taxonomy
- Restored required ### policy_agent subsection structure under sections 1-4 - Reclassified value-boundary coverage gaps out of Boundary Violations - Normalized Entry 39 metadata and added Entry 40 for the correction milestone
